  • Cell

  • API Reference
  • Membrane and Ionomer

Membrane and Ionomer#

The membrane subsystem has two separate class hierarchies that are composed rather than merged:

Ionomer — transport correlations for the polymer electrolyte phase, used by both the membrane and the catalyst layers. Ionomer is the abstract base; PFSAIonomer provides empirical fits for Nafion-type ionomers (proton conductivity, O₂ permeability, H₂ permeability, electroosmotic drag, and water diffusivity).

Membrane — geometric properties (dry thickness) and water-balance correlations at the membrane level. Membrane delegates transport correlations to its composed ionomer. PFSA specialises to PFSA membranes and adds the Springer et al. (1991) vapor-equilibrium isotherm and liquid equilibrium.

Water balance models — solve the 1-D water-content profile across the membrane. Two implementations are provided:

  • MembraneWaterBalanceModelPiecewise (default) — boundary conditions derived from a piecewise-linear regression of the equilibrium isotherm RH(λ) stored on the ionomer.

  • MembraneWaterBalanceModel — boundary conditions derived from the first-order linear expansion of the isotherm used in Affonso Nobrega et al. (2026).

Both are orchestrated by WaterBalanceModel, which also handles gas-transport resistances and liquid saturation.

Ionomer#

class marapendi.membrane.ionomer_base.Ionomer(equivalent_weight, dry_density, vapor_equilibrium_polynomial, reference_conductivity, reference_water_diffusivity, reference_water_absorption_coefficient, conductivity_exp, conductivity_fv_threshold, hydrated_proton_conductivity, conductivity_activation_energy, water_diffusivity_activation_energy, water_absorption_activation_energy, reference_conductivity_temperature, reference_water_absorption_temperature, reference_water_diffusivity_temperature)[source]#

Bases: object

Dry material properties of an ion-exchange polymer.

Parameters:

  • equivalent_weight (float)

  • dry_density (float)

  • vapor_equilibrium_polynomial (ndarray)

  • reference_conductivity (float)

  • reference_water_diffusivity (float)

  • reference_water_absorption_coefficient (float)

  • conductivity_exp (float)

  • conductivity_fv_threshold (float)

  • hydrated_proton_conductivity (float)

  • conductivity_activation_energy (float)

  • water_diffusivity_activation_energy (float)

  • water_absorption_activation_energy (float)

  • reference_conductivity_temperature (float)

  • reference_water_absorption_temperature (float)

  • reference_water_diffusivity_temperature (float)

equivalent_weight#

Equivalent weight (kg/kmol).

Type:

float

dry_density#

Dry density (kg/m^3).

Type:

float

reference_water_diffusivity#

Reference adsorbed-water diffusivity (m^2/s).

Type:

float

reference_absorption_coefficient#

Reference water absorption coefficient (m/s).

Type:

float

reference_temperature#

Reference temperature for the Arrhenius corrections (K).

Type:

float

water_diffusivity_activation_energy, water_absorption_activation_energy

Activation energies for water diffusivity / absorption (J/kmol).

Type:

float

calculate_electroosmotic_drag_coefficient(temperature, water_content)[source]#

Electroosmotic drag coefficient (n.d.) for a given water_content.

Parameters:

  • temperature (float)

  • water_content (float)

Return type:

float

calculate_electroosmotic_drag_speed(temperature, current_density)[source]#

Electroosmotic drag speed (m/s) for a given current_density (A/m^2).

Parameters:

  • temperature (float)

  • current_density (float)

Return type:

float

calculate_water_absorption_coefficient(temperature)[source]#

Water absorption coefficient (m/s) at temperature.

Parameters:

temperature (float)

Return type:

float

calculate_water_diffusivity(temperature)[source]#

Adsorbed water diffusivity (m^2/s) at temperature.

Parameters:

temperature (float)

Return type:

float

charge_conductivity(water_content, temperature, charge='proton')[source]#

Charge conductivity (proton or hydroxide), in S/m.

Dispatches to proton_conductivity() or hydroxide_conductivity(), which must be implemented by concrete subclasses.

Parameters:

  • water_content (float)

  • temperature (float)

  • charge (str)

Return type:

float

conductivity_exp: float#
conductivity_fv_threshold: float#
dry_density: float#

hydrated_proton_conductivity: float#

reference_water_absorption_coefficient: float#
reference_water_absorption_temperature: float#
reference_water_diffusivity: float#
reference_water_diffusivity_temperature: float#
vapor_equilibrium_polynomial: ndarray#
water_absorption_activation_energy: float#
water_diffusivity_activation_energy: float#
water_vol_fraction(water_content, water_molar_volume)[source]#

Volume fraction of water in the ionomer, for a given water_content (mol H2O / mol SO3-).

Parameters:

  • water_content (float)

  • water_molar_volume (float)

Return type:

float

wet_density(water_content, temperature)[source]#

Wet density of the ionomer (kg/m^3).

Parameters:

  • water_content (float)

  • temperature (float)

Return type:

float

wet_expansion_factor(water_content, temperature)[source]#

Volumetric expansion factor due to water uptake, relative to the dry volume.

Parameters:

  • water_content (float)

  • temperature (float)

Return type:

float

class marapendi.membrane.pem.PFSAIonomer(equivalent_weight=952.0, dry_density=2004.0, vapor_equilibrium_polynomial=<factory>, reference_conductivity=50, reference_water_diffusivity=4.3e-10, reference_water_absorption_coefficient=1e-05, conductivity_exp=1.5, conductivity_fv_threshold=0.11, hydrated_proton_conductivity=11, conductivity_activation_energy=11000000.0, water_diffusivity_activation_energy=20000000.0, water_absorption_activation_energy=20000000.0, reference_conductivity_temperature=353.15, reference_water_absorption_temperature=353.15, reference_water_diffusivity_temperature=353.15, conductivity_correction=1.0, hydrated_o2_diffusion=7.430439362572494e-10, o2_diffusion_exponent=0.708, o2_diffusion_activation_energy=24000000.0)[source]#

Bases: Ionomer

PFSA ionomer (e.g. Nafion) with empirical fits for conductivity and O₂ transport.

Parameters:

  • equivalent_weight (float)

  • dry_density (float)

  • vapor_equilibrium_polynomial (list)

  • reference_conductivity (float)

  • reference_water_diffusivity (float)

  • reference_water_absorption_coefficient (float)

  • conductivity_exp (float)

  • conductivity_fv_threshold (float)

  • hydrated_proton_conductivity (float)

  • conductivity_activation_energy (float)

  • water_diffusivity_activation_energy (float)

  • water_absorption_activation_energy (float)

  • reference_conductivity_temperature (float)

  • reference_water_absorption_temperature (float)

  • reference_water_diffusivity_temperature (float)

  • conductivity_correction (float)

  • hydrated_o2_diffusion (float)

  • o2_diffusion_exponent (float)

  • o2_diffusion_activation_energy (float)

equivalent_weight#

Equivalent weight (kg/kmol, i.e. g/mol). Default 952 corresponds to Nafion NR-211/N-115.

Type:

float

dry_density#

Dry polymer density (kg/m³).

Type:

float

vapor_equilibrium_polynomial#

Coefficients [a, b, c, d] of the cubic λ(RH) isotherm λ = ((a·RH + b)·RH + c)·RH + d.

Type:

list of float

conductivity_correction#

Multiplicative correction on the reference conductivity (used for calibration; default 1.0 leaves the correlation unchanged).

Type:

float

reference_conductivity#

Pre-exponential coefficient in the proton conductivity correlation (S/m).

Type:

float

conductivity_exp#

Free-volume exponent in the conductivity correlation.

Type:

float

conductivity_activation_energy#

Activation energy for proton conductivity (J/kmol).

Type:

float

reference_conductivity_temperature#

Reference temperature for the conductivity Arrhenius factor (K).

Type:

float

hydrated_o2_diffusion#

O₂ diffusivity in fully hydrated ionomer at the reference temperature (m²/s).

Type:

float

o2_diffusion_exponent#

Water-content exponent for O₂ diffusivity.

Type:

float

o2_diffusion_activation_energy#

Activation energy for O₂ diffusion in ionomer (J/kmol).

Type:

float

calculate_electroosmotic_drag_coefficient(temperature, water_content)[source]#

Electroosmotic drag coefficient (n.d.) for a given water_content.

Parameters:

  • temperature (float)

  • water_content (float)

Return type:

float

conductivity_activation_energy: float = 11000000.0#
conductivity_correction: float = 1.0#
conductivity_exp: float = 1.5#
conductivity_fv_threshold: float = 0.11#
dry_density: float = 2004.0#
equivalent_weight: float = 952.0#
fit_rh_piecewise_linear(n_segments=3, temperature=353.15, rh_breaks=None)[source]#

Fit and cache a continuous piecewise linear approximation of RH(λ_eq).

The procedure has two distinct steps:

  1. Fitting intervals (in RH space, given by rh_breaks): each segment gets a least-squares line RH = a_k · λ + b_k fitted on the data in its interval.

  2. Validity intervals (in λ space): adjacent lines are extended until they intersect; each line is valid between the two nearest intersections. Continuity is guaranteed at every transition.

When rh_breaks is None, interior fitting-interval boundaries are optimised to minimise the RMS approximation error under the intersection-based validity scheme.

Results stored on self#

fit_rh_breaksndarray, shape (n_segments + 1,)

Fitting-interval boundaries in RH space (0 … 1).

lmbd_pwl_breaksndarray, shape (n_segments + 1,)

Validity-interval boundaries in λ space (domain endpoints + pairwise line intersections).

rh_pwl_breaksndarray, shape (n_segments + 1,)

RH values at the validity boundaries (equal from both sides by construction — continuity check).

pwl_slopesndarray, shape (n_segments,)

Regression slope a_k for each segment.

pwl_interceptsndarray, shape (n_segments,)

Regression intercept b_k for each segment.

pwl_temperaturefloat

Temperature (K) used for fitting.

Parameters:

  • n_segments (int)

  • temperature (float)

Return type:

None

h2_permeability(water_content, temperature=353.15)[source]#

H2 permeability (kmol/m/s/Pa) from a volume-fraction approach.

References

Goshtasbi, A. et al. J. Electrochem. Soc. 167, 024518 (2020).

Parameters:

  • water_content (float)

  • temperature (float)

Return type:

float

hydrated_o2_diffusion: float = 7.430439362572494e-10#
hydrated_proton_conductivity: float = 11#
hydroxide_conductivity(water_content, temperature)[source]#

Hydroxide conductivity (S/m). PFSA ionomers do not conduct hydroxide.

Parameters:

  • water_content (float)

  • temperature (float)

Return type:

float

linear_rh_from_water_content(lmbd, interval=None)[source]#

Evaluate the piecewise linear approximation RH(λ_eq).

Parameters:

  • lmbd (array_like) – Water content λ (mol H₂O / mol SO₃⁻).

  • interval (int or None) –

    • None (default): select the segment automatically from the validity intervals (line intersections). Result is clipped to [0, 1].

    • k (integer, supports negative indexing): always use segment k’s regression line regardless of the value of lmbd. No clipping applied — useful for deliberate extrapolation.

Return type:

ndarray

linear_water_content_from_rh(rh, interval=None)[source]#

Evaluate the inverse piecewise linear approximation λ_eq(RH).

Inverts RH = a_k · λ + b_k → λ = (RH − b_k) / a_k.

Parameters:

  • rh (array_like) – Relative humidity (0–1).

  • interval (int or None) –

    • None (default): select the segment automatically from the validity intervals in RH space. No clipping applied.

    • k (integer, supports negative indexing): always use segment k’s regression line regardless of the value of rh.

Return type:

ndarray

liquid_equilibrium_water_content(temperature)[source]#

Equilibrium water content in contact with liquid water.

References

Goshtasbi, A. et al. J. Electrochem. Soc. 167, 024518 (2020).

o2_diffusion_activation_energy: float = 24000000.0#
o2_diffusion_exponent: float = 0.708#
o2_film_diffusion_coefficient(water_content, temperature=353.15)[source]#

Effective O2 diffusion coefficient in the hydrated ionomer film (m^2/s).

Parameters:

  • water_content (float)

  • temperature (float)

Return type:

float

o2_permeability(water_content, temperature=353.15)[source]#

O2 permeability (kmol/m/s/Pa) from a volume-fraction approach.

References

Goshtasbi, A. et al. J. Electrochem. Soc. 167, 024518 (2020).

Parameters:

  • water_content (float)

  • temperature (float)

Return type:

float

proton_conductivity(water_content, temperature)[source]#

Proton conductivity from empirical fits (S/m).

Parameters:

  • water_content (float)

  • temperature (float)

Return type:

float

reference_conductivity: float = 50#
reference_conductivity_temperature: float = 353.15#
reference_water_absorption_coefficient: float = 1e-05#
reference_water_absorption_temperature: float = 353.15#
reference_water_diffusivity: float = 4.3e-10#
reference_water_diffusivity_temperature: float = 353.15#
vapor_equilibrium_polynomial: list#
vapor_equilibrium_water_content(rh, temperature)[source]#

Equilibrium water content λ as a function of relative humidity.

Evaluates the cubic polynomial λ = ((a₀·RH + a₁)·RH + a₂)·RH + a₃ defined by vapor_equilibrium_polynomial.

Parameters:

  • rh (array_like) – Relative humidity (0–1).

  • temperature (float) – Temperature (K). Not used by the polynomial form but kept for interface consistency with subclasses.

Returns:

Water content λ (mol H₂O / mol SO₃⁻).

Return type:

float or ndarray

References

Springer, T. E. et al. J. Electrochem. Soc. 138, 2334 (1991).

vapor_equilibrium_water_content_derivative(rh, temperature)[source]#

Derivative of the vapor equilibrium water content with respect to RH (mol H₂O / mol SO₃⁻).

water_absorption_activation_energy: float = 20000000.0#
water_diffusivity_activation_energy: float = 20000000.0#

Membrane#

class marapendi.membrane.membrane_base.Membrane(dry_thickness=2.5e-05, ionomer=None)[source]#

Bases: object

Static properties of a proton/anion exchange membrane.

Holds membrane geometry and delegates transport correlations to the composed ionomer.

Parameters:

  • dry_thickness (float)

  • ionomer (Ionomer)

dry_thickness#

Membrane thickness in the dry state (m).

Type:

float

ionomer#

Ionomer instance providing transport correlations.

Type:

Ionomer

calculate_electroosmotic_drag_speed(temperature, current_density)[source]#

Electroosmotic drag velocity (m/s) — delegates to ionomer.

Parameters:

  • temperature (float)

  • current_density (float)

Return type:

float

calculate_water_absorption_coefficient(temperature)[source]#

Water absorption coefficient (m/s) — delegates to ionomer.

Parameters:

temperature (float)

Return type:

float

calculate_water_diffusivity(temperature)[source]#

Adsorbed-water diffusivity (m²/s) — delegates to ionomer.

Parameters:

temperature (float)

Return type:

float

charge_resistance(water_content, temperature, charge='proton')[source]#

Through-plane charge resistance (Ω·m²).

property dry_concentration: float#

Dry molar concentration of ion-exchange sites (kmol/m³).

dry_thickness: float = 2.5e-05#
hydrogen_permeation_flux(h2_pressure_difference, temperature, water_content)[source]#

Hydrogen permeation flux through the membrane (kmol/m²/s).

Parameters:

  • h2_pressure_difference (float)

  • temperature (float)

  • water_content (float)

Return type:

float

ionomer: Ionomer = None#
class marapendi.membrane.pem.PFSA(dry_thickness=2.5e-05, ionomer=<factory>, relaxation_time_constant=0.067, relaxation_time_activation_energy=28000000.0, uptake_relaxed_fraction_constant=0.014, phi=0.15)[source]#

Bases: Membrane

Perfluorosulfonic-acid (PFSA, e.g. Nafion) membrane.

Parameters:

  • dry_thickness (float)

  • ionomer (PFSAIonomer)

  • relaxation_time_constant (float)

  • relaxation_time_activation_energy (float)

  • uptake_relaxed_fraction_constant (float)

  • phi (float)

conductivity_correction#

Correction factor for the proton conductivity correlation (Vetter and Schumacher, 2020).

Type:

float

conductivity_exp#

Exponent of the proton conductivity correlation.

Type:

float

conductivity_activation_energy#

Activation energy for proton conductivity (J/kmol).

Type:

float

phi#

Contribution of relaxation phenomena to the ionomer water uptake, according to Goshtasbi et al. (2019).

Type:

float

References

Springer, T. E. et al. J. Electrochem. Soc. 138, 2334 (1991). Goshtasbi, A. et al. J. Electrochem. Soc. 167, 024518 (2020).

equilibrium_water_content(rh, temperature, s_relax=None)[source]#

Equilibrium water content from the Springer et al. (1991) polynomial isotherm.

References

Springer, T. E. et al. J. Electrochem. Soc. 138, 2334 (1991). Goshtasbi et al. J. Electrochem. Soc. 2019, 166 (7), F3154.

equilibrium_water_content_derivative(rh, temperature, s_relax=None)[source]#

Derivative of equilibrium water content with respect to RH, accounting for relaxation.

liquid_equilibrium_water_content(temperature)[source]#

Equilibrium water content in contact with liquid water — delegates to ionomer.

phi: float = 0.15#
proton_conductivity(water_content_profile, temperature)[source]#

Effective through-plane proton conductivity (S/m).

Computes the harmonic mean of the local conductivities across the water-content profile, consistent with a series resistance model.

Parameters:

  • water_content_profile (array_like) – Water content λ at each mesh point along the membrane thickness.

  • temperature (float) – Membrane temperature (K).

Returns:

Harmonic-mean proton conductivity (S/m).

Return type:

float or ndarray

proton_resistance(state, water_saturation=0)[source]#

Through-plane proton resistance (Ω·m²).

Weights liquid- and vapor-equilibrated conductivities by water saturation.

relaxation_time_activation_energy: float = 28000000.0#
relaxation_time_constant: float = 0.067#
uptake_relaxed_fraction_constant: float = 0.014#

Water balance models#

class marapendi.water_balance.water_balance.WaterBalanceModel(sorption_activity_driving_force=False, eod_parallel_to_sorption=False, membrane_water_balance_model=<factory>)[source]#

Bases: object

A class representing a membrane water balance model with various parameters related to water diffusivity and absorption.

Parameters:

  • sorption_activity_driving_force (bool)

  • eod_parallel_to_sorption (bool)

  • membrane_water_balance_model (MembraneWaterBalanceModel)

sorption_activity_driving_force#

Boolean flag indicating whether water activity is the driving force for water absorption (default is False). If false, water content difference is considered as the driving force.

Type:

bool, optional

eod_parallel_to_sorption#

Boolean flag indicating if electro-osmotic drag is parallel to water absorption/desorption (default is False). If True, electro-osmotic drag is added to the water absorption flux on the RHS of the water balance boundary conditions.

Type:

bool, optional

Notes

Based on Ferrara et al. (2018), accounting for gas transport resistance and non-equilibrium sorption at the membrane interface.

References

Ferrara, A. et al. J. Power Sources 390, 197–207 (2018).

calculate_water_saturation(cell_side, side_state, calculate_cl_saturation=True)[source]#

Compute and update water saturation in each porous layer.

Parameters:

  • cell_side (FuelCellSide) – Component side — provides static layer parameters (contact angle, two-phase transport model, has_gdl, has_mpl).

  • side_state (CellSideState) – Runtime state for this side — liquid_flux and gas_flux must already be set; saturation fields are written to each layer state.

  • calculate_cl_saturation (bool, optional) – When False, the catalyst-layer saturation is not recomputed (used when the CL saturation is prescribed externally, e.g. in transient models). Default is True.

Return type:

None

calculate_water_transport(cell, state, dynamic=False, water_profile=None, gas_transport_model=None)[source]#

Calculate the water balance across the fuel cell.

Updates vapor transport resistances, solves the membrane water balance, and (when dynamic=False) recalculates liquid saturation in the cathode.

Parameters:

  • cell (FuelCell) – Component tree providing static physics and legacy state attributes.

  • state (CellState) – Runtime state: h2ov transport resistances and water-flux fields are written here and also synced to the component tree for backward compatibility.

  • dynamic (bool) – When True, skips the liquid saturation update (used by transient models that prescribe the water profile externally).

  • water_profile (np.ndarray, optional) – Prescribed membrane water-content profile (shape (n_mesh, ...)). Passed through to MembraneWaterBalanceModel.solve_membrane_water_balance(); when None the steady-state profile is computed internally.

  • gas_transport_model (GasTransportModel, optional) – Shared instance for computing H₂O vapor transport resistances. A temporary instance is created if not provided.

Return type:

None

eod_parallel_to_sorption: bool = False#
membrane_water_balance_model: MembraneWaterBalanceModel#
membrane_water_rate_of_change(cell, state, n_memb_mesh)[source]#

Rate of change of water content at each membrane mesh node (1/s).

Uses a finite-volume discretisation of the combined diffusion + EOD flux divergence, with boundary fluxes taken from the absorption/desorption terms already stored in state by calculate_water_transport().

Parameters:

  • cell (FuelCell)

  • state (CellState) – Must have membrane.water_content_profile, membrane.peclet_number, membrane.water_diffusion_resistance, an.membrane_water_flux, and ca.membrane_water_flux already populated.

  • n_memb_mesh (int) – Number of membrane mesh nodes (must match len(state.membrane.water_content_profile)).

Return type:

np.ndarray, shape (n_memb_mesh, …)

relaxation_rate_of_change(cell, state)[source]#

Rate of change of the ionomer sorption relaxation variable on each side (1/s).

Returns a 2-element list [dsrelax_ca_dt, dsrelax_an_dt]. Requires state.*.s_relax and state.*.rh_at_cl_without_crossover to be set.

sorption_activity_driving_force: bool = False#
update_cell_side_water_fluxes(side_state)[source]#

Split total water flux into liquid and vapor components.

Liquid flux = excess above the maximum vapor removal capacity of the channel (at the current saturation state of the CL).

Return type:

None

class marapendi.water_balance.membrane_pwl.MembraneWaterBalanceModelPiecewise(n_profile_points=10, sorption_activity_driving_force=False, eod_parallel_to_sorption=False)[source]#

Bases: MembraneWaterBalanceModel

Membrane water balance using a piecewise linear sorption isotherm.

The linear segment active at each operating point is found by iterating downward from the highest segment until the resulting equilibrium water content falls within the validity bounds of the chosen segment. Convergence is guaranteed in at most n_segments iterations.

Parameters:

  • n_profile_points (int)

  • sorption_activity_driving_force (bool)

  • eod_parallel_to_sorption (bool)

estimate_equilibrium_water_contents(cell, state)[source]#

Estimate CL equilibrium water content using the piecewise linear λ(RH) inverse.

side_state.pwl_interval selects which linear segment to use. This must already be set (by solve_membrane_water_balance()) before calling this method.

solve_membrane_water_balance(cell, state=None, water_profile=None, dynamic=False)[source]#

Solve the membrane water balance using piecewise linear λ(RH).

The linear segment is chosen self-consistently: after solving with a candidate segment the resulting eq_water_content is tested against the validity intervals (line intersections). If it falls outside the assumed segment the nearest correct segment is selected and the solve is repeated. Convergence is guaranteed for a monotone isotherm in at most n_segments iterations.

Parameters:

  • cell (FuelCell)

  • state (CellState)

  • water_profile (np.ndarray, optional) – Required when dynamic is True.

  • dynamic (bool) – If True solve for a prescribed water profile (transient mode).

update_non_dim_vapor_resistance(side_state, memb_state, cell)[source]#

Non-dimensional vapor resistance R_v* for one electrode side (Ferrara et al. 2018, eq. 13).

class marapendi.water_balance.membrane.MembraneWaterBalanceModel(n_profile_points=10, sorption_activity_driving_force=False, eod_parallel_to_sorption=False)[source]#

Bases: object

Steady-state membrane water balance using an analytical profile solution.

Solves the combined diffusion and electroosmotic drag problem analytically, computing the water-content profile, boundary absorption/desorption fluxes, and equilibrium water content at each catalyst layer interface.

Parameters:

  • n_profile_points (int)

  • sorption_activity_driving_force (bool)

  • eod_parallel_to_sorption (bool)

n_profile_points#

Number of points used to discretise the membrane profile for output and downstream calculations.

Type:

int

sorption_activity_driving_force#

When True, water activity is the driving force for sorption at the membrane surface; otherwise water content difference is used.

Type:

bool

eod_parallel_to_sorption#

When True, the electroosmotic drag contribution is added to the absorption flux in the boundary conditions.

Type:

bool

calculate_biot_number(absorption_coefficient, membrane_thickness, water_diffusivity)[source]#

Calculate the Biot number using absorption coefficient, membrane thickness, and water diffusivity.

Parameters:

  • absorption_coefficient (float) – Water absorption coefficient at the membrane surface (m/s).

  • membrane_thickness (float) – Membrane dry thickness (m).

  • water_diffusivity (float) – Adsorbed water diffusivity (m²/s).

Returns:

The calculated Biot number, dimensionless.

Return type:

float

calculate_membrane_transport_properties(cell, state)[source]#

Compute EOD speed, absorption coefficient, water diffusivity, and diffusion resistance; write to state.membrane.

calculate_peclet_number(eod_speed, membrane_thickness, water_diffusivity)[source]#

Calculate the Peclet number using electroosmotic drag speed, membrane thickness, and water diffusivity.

Parameters:

  • eod_speed (float) – Electroosmotic drag velocity (m/s).

  • membrane_thickness (float) – Membrane dry thickness (m).

  • water_diffusivity (float) – Adsorbed water diffusivity (m²/s).

Returns:

The calculated Peclet number, dimensionless.

Return type:

float

eod_parallel_to_sorption: bool = False#
equilibrium_water_content_from_estimated(state)[source]#
estimate_equilibrium_water_contents(cell, state)[source]#

Estimate the CL equilibrium water content and its derivative for both sides.

Reads gas state from state via GasModel so that the saturation pressure (already stored on each state.*.cl by ThermalModel.set_mea_temperature()) is reused without being recomputed.

Parameters:

  • cell (FuelCell) – Provides the membrane physics (equilibrium water content model).

  • state (CellState) – Runtime state whose side states carry pre-computed saturation_pressure on their catalyst-layer sub-states.

n_profile_points: int = 10#
solve_membrane_water_balance(cell, state=None, water_profile=None, dynamic=False)[source]#

Calculate and update the water balance properties in the cell.

Parameters:

  • cell (FuelCell) – Cell object containing membrane, cathode (ca), and anode (an) components.

  • state (CellState, optional) – When provided, gas reads in estimate_equilibrium_water_contents() use the pre-computed saturation pressures on state.*.cl rather than recomputing them. Results are also written to state.sides.

  • water_profile (np.ndarray, optional) – Initial water content profile, required when dynamic=True.

  • dynamic (bool) – If True, solves for a prescribed water profile without updating fluxes.

sorption_activity_driving_force: bool = False#
update_membrane_water_fluxes(state)[source]#

Calculate membrane water fluxes for both sides. Reads/writes state.

update_non_dim_vapor_resistance(side_state, memb_state, cell)[source]#

Non-dimensional vapor resistance R_v* for one electrode side (Ferrara et al. 2018, eq. 13).

update_non_dimensional_parameters(cell, state, pwl_interval=None)[source]#

Calculate various non-dimensional parameters related to water transport and equilibrium in a cell. Reads runtime values from state; writes results to state and syncs to cell for methods that still read from the component tree.

update_water_contents(state)[source]#

Set membrane and CL water contents. Writes to state.

update_water_profile(state)[source]#

Calculate the water content profile across the membrane. Reads from state.

previous

Flow Channels
